W (l) unnoas-qns-s "09099-11 LHVd \ (I) m-ma—s gun—~11 hits Auvmaflovmm 011wr22021122192 2112 66/vOOSS—0k3h 01:2 041 198241 Edmfiéfif -- 1 of 2 -- 2 THE GAZETTE 0F INDIA:EXTRAORDINARY [PART II—Src, 3(1)] MINISTRY OF RAILWAYS of pension, if any, drawn or entitled to be drawn while holding office in the Tribunal shall not exceed (RAILWAY BOARD) rupees two lakh thirty four thousand per annum”. NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 29th August, 2008 G.S.R. 625 (E).—ln exercise ofthe powers conferred by sub—section (1), read with clausel(b) of sub-section (2), of Section 30 and Section 30A, of the Railway Claims Tribunal Act, 1987 (54 of 1987), the Central Government hereby makes the following rules further to amend the Rail- way Claims Tribunal (Salaries and Allowances and Conditions of Service of Chairman, Vice-Chairman and Members) Rules, 1989, namely :— 1. Short title and c0mmeneement.—-(1) These rules may be called the Railway Claims Tribunal (Salaries and Allowances and Conditions of Service of Chairman, Vice-Chairman and Members) Amendment Rules, 2008. (2) They shall be deemed to have come into force with effect from the lst April, 2004. 2. Amendment ofrule 8.-In the Railway Claims Tribunal (Salaries and Allowances and Conditions of Service of Chairman, Vice-Chairman and Members) Rules, 1989, in rule 8, for sub-rule (2), the following sub-rule shall be substituted, namely :— “(2) Pension under sub-rule ( 1) shall be calculated at the rate of rupees four thousand seven hundred and sixteen per annum for each completed year ofservice subject to the condition that the aggregate amount of pension payable under this rule, together with the amount of any pension including commuted portion [No. 94/1"C (RCT)/2-2 PL] SUNIL KUMAR, Executive Director, Public Grievances EXPLANATORY MEMORANDUM In View of merger of 50% of Dearness Allowances with basic pay of the Central Government employees for the purpose of calculation of pension with effect from the lst April, 2004, it has been decided to revise the upper limit of pension payable to the Chairman, Vice—Chairman and Members of the Tribunal with effect from the lst April, 2004. It is, therefore, proposed to amend sub—rule (2) of rule 8 of the Railway Claims Tribunal (Salaries and Allowances and Conditions of Service of Chairman, Vice-Chairman and Members) Rules, 1989 with effect from the lst April, 2004 i.e. with retrospective effect. 2. It is certified that no person shall be adversely affected by the aforesaid amendment being given retrospective effect. Footnote: The Railway Claims Tribrmal (Salaries and Allowances and Conditions of Service of Chairman, Vice—Chairman and Members) Rules, 1989, published in the Gazette of India, Vida, notification No. G.S.Rl 844(E), dated 19th September, 1989 and subsequently amended by (1) G.S.R. 726(E), dated 6th December, 1991, (2) G.S.R. 18503), dated 11th April, 1996, (3) G.S.R. 733(E), dated 2lst September, 2000 and (4) G.S.R, 386(B), dated 25th May, 2001 .
